title: Song for "The Jacquerie".
content:
May the maiden,
    Violet-laden
    Out of the violet sea,
    Comes and hovers
    Over lovers,
    Over thee, Marie, and me,
    Over me and thee.

    Day the stately,
    Sunken lately
    Into the violet sea,
    Backward hovers
    Over lovers,
    Over thee, Marie, and me,
    Over me and thee.

    Night the holy,
    Sailing slowly
    Over the violet sea,
    Stars uncovers
    Over lovers,
    Stars for thee, Marie, and me,
    Stars for me and thee.


    Macon, Georgia, 1868.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Sidney Lanier delivers a powerful performance in "Song for "The Jacquerie"."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
